sources.notes: Statement and constraints: construct a binary string with prescribed counts and exact maximum substring imbalance; total output length is bounded by 4e5.
Official editorial and embedded construction checked for the feasibility bounds and dominant-block plus alternation construction.
Cross-checked the same feasibility interval and construction, including zero-count boundary cases.
Re-derived the prefix-walk range identity and exhaustively checked the construction for all n,m <= 6 and all k.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution index; the concrete consulted article is listed in sources.

annotation.summary: Construct a binary string with exactly n zeroes and m ones whose largest absolute zero-one count difference over every contiguous substring is exactly k, or report impossibility.
annotation.constraints: Up to 1e4 tests; 0 <= n,m <= 2e5, n+m >= 1, 1 <= k <= n+m, and total counts per symbol are at most 2e5, so construction must be linear in output length.
annotation.core_idea: Map 0 to +1 and 1 to -1. A substring imbalance is the difference of two prefix sums, so the maximum is max(P)-min(P). Necessarily |n-m| <= k because the whole string is a substring, and k <= max(n,m) because no substring can have larger excess than the available count of one symbol. These are sufficient. WLOG n>=m, let r=n-k and u=m-r=k-(n-m), then output 0^k (10)^r 1^u. Prefix sums rise to k, alternate between k and k-1, then decrease to n-m without going below 0, giving range exactly k. If m>n, complement the symmetric construction.
annotation.complexity: O(n+m) time and O(1) auxiliary space excluding output.
tricks: turn substring imbalance into a prefix-sum range
attain k with a dominant block and cancel the remainder by alternation

solution_variants.name: prefix-range-block-alternation
solution_variants.summary: Emit k dominant symbols, pair each remaining dominant symbol with one minority symbol, append leftover minority symbols, and complement when ones dominate.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n+m) time and O(1) auxiliary space.

tags.evidence: An explicit count-preserving binary-string construction realizes the exact target prefix-range width.
Maximum substring imbalance becomes max prefix sum minus min prefix sum after mapping bits to +1/-1.
After forcing one excursion of size k, alternation cancels remaining symbols without leaving the chosen interval.
Swapping zero and one negates prefix sums without changing their range.
The output is a string, but no string algorithm is used.
